protocol.name: ext_background_effect_v1
protocol.copyright:

interface.name: ext_background_effect_manager_v1
interface.description:
This protocol provides a way to improve visuals of translucent surfaces by applying effects like blur to the background behind them.
The capabilities are send when the global is bound, and every time they change. Note that when the capability goes away, the corresponding effect is no longer applied by the compositor, even if it was set before.
Warning! The protocol described in this file is currently in the testing phase. Backward compatible changes may be added together with the corresponding interface version bump. Backward incompatible changes can only be done by creating a new major version of the extension.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Informs the server that the client will no longer be using this protocol object. Existing objects created by this object are not affected.

entry.name: background_effect_exists
entry.summary: the surface already has a background effect object

request.name: get_background_effect
request.description:
Instantiate an interface extension for the given wl_surface to add effects like blur for the background behind it.
If the given wl_surface already has a ext_background_effect_surface_v1 object associated, the background_effect_exists protocol error will be raised.

interface.name: ext_background_effect_surface_v1
interface.description:
The background effect object provides a way to specify a region behind a surface that should have background effects like blur applied.
If the wl_surface associated with the ext_background_effect_surface_v1 object has been destroyed, this object becomes inert.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Informs the server that the client will no longer be using this protocol object. The effect regions will be removed on the next commit.

entry.name: surface_destroyed
entry.summary: the associated surface has been destroyed

request.name: set_blur_region
request.description:
This request sets the region of the surface that will have its background blurred.
The blur region is specified in the surface-local coordinates, and clipped by the compositor to the surface size.
The initial value for the blur region is empty. Setting the pending blur region has copy semantics, and the wl_region object can be destroyed immediately. A NULL wl_region removes the effect.
The blur region is double-buffered state, and will be applied on the next wl_surface.commit.
The blur algorithm is subject to compositor policies.
If the associated surface has been destroyed, the surface_destroyed error will be raised.

request.name: set_blur_radius
request.description:
This request sets the preferred blur radius for the background effect. The value is specified in surface-local logical pixels.
The blur radius is double-buffered state, and will be applied on the next wl_surface.commit.

request.name: set_tint
request.description:
This request sets the tint applied after the background effect. Colors are encoded as RGBA with the red channel in the most significant byte and alpha in the least significant byte.
The tint is double-buffered state, and will be applied on the next wl_surface.commit.

request.name: set_border
request.description:
This request sets the border color applied around the effect region. Colors are encoded as RGBA with the red channel in the most significant byte and alpha in the least significant byte.
The border is double-buffered state, and will be applied on the next wl_surface.commit.

request.name: set_corner_radii
request.description:
This request sets the corner radii for the effect region in surface-local logical pixels. The arguments are ordered clockwise from top-left.
The corner radii are double-buffered state, and will be applied on the next wl_surface.commit.

request.name: set_base_color
request.description:
This request sets the base material wash applied after the background effect and before the tint. Colors are encoded as RGBA with the red channel in the most significant byte and alpha in the least significant byte.
The base color is double-buffered state, and will be applied on the next wl_surface.commit.

entry.name: below
entry.summary: arrow above the card, content below anchor

entry.name: above
entry.summary: arrow below the card, content above anchor

entry.name: end
entry.summary: arrow on the leading edge, content after anchor

entry.name: start
entry.summary: arrow on the trailing edge, content before anchor

request.name: set_shape
request.description:
This request sets the shape used to draw the background material and border for the effect region. The default is rounded_rect. For callout, placement describes which side of the card the arrow is attached to.
The shape is double-buffered state, and will be applied on the next wl_surface.commit.
